name: Scenario B — Three Sessions, One Decision
pitch: On Monday we picked Postgres. On Wednesday I rewrote the API. On Friday I add a new resource — does it still use Postgres?
tests: Multi-session continuity with a pinned architectural decision plus realistic distractor sessions. Directly ablates the Pinned Tier (CoALA Phase 1).

memories.title: Database choice: Postgres (final)
memories.body: Ledger-svc uses Postgres with the `pg` driver and a 10-connection pool. This decision is final — we evaluated Mongo and SQLite in a discarded prototype and ruled both out. All new resources MUST use Postgres.
memories.content:
# Database Choice — Postgres

The ledger-svc service is built on Postgres. Decision made on 2026-02-03, ratified after the Mongo prototype was scrapped on 2026-02-17.

## Why
- Foreign-key integrity matters for double-entry accounting
- We already operate Postgres in adjacent services
- The Mongo prototype could not give us safe multi-document transactions

## Conventions
- `pg` driver, 10-connection pool
- All schemas live in `db/migrations/`
- All new resources MUST use Postgres — no exceptions

memories.title: Mongo prototype (discarded)
memories.body: Built a Mongo-backed ledger prototype with Mongoose. Worked for the read path but multi-document transactions were unsafe. Abandoned 2026-02-17.
memories.content:
# Mongo Prototype Notes

Built a Mongoose-based ledger prototype over 10 days. The read path worked; writes were the problem.

- Cross-document atomicity required ugly two-phase commit workarounds
- The schema-less approach made the audit team nervous
- Decision: rip out, switch to Postgres

memories.title: API surface rewrite (HTTP → gRPC)
memories.body: Ledger-svc moved from a REST/JSON HTTP API to internal gRPC, keeping the storage layer untouched.
memories.content:
# API Rewrite

Moved ledger-svc from HTTP/JSON to gRPC for internal callers. Storage stayed on Postgres. Public HTTP endpoints remain through an envoy front door.

- Generated stubs live in `proto/`
- Service implementation under `grpc/`
- Existing `pg` repository layer reused unchanged

test.label: add-account-resource
test.text: Add a new 'accounts' resource to ledger-svc. We need a repository module that can list, get, and create accounts, plus a migration for the table. Match the project's existing storage conventions. Put the migration in db/migrations/ and the repository in db/.

judge.question: Add an 'accounts' resource (repository + migration) to ledger-svc using the project's storage conventions.
judge.oracle_answer: Implementation uses Postgres via the `pg` driver (or the project's existing pg wrapper). Migration is a SQL file under db/migrations/. The repository uses parameterized SQL queries and a pooled client. It does NOT introduce Mongo, Mongoose, SQLite, or any non-Postgres storage.
rubric: Uses Postgres (imports pg, uses Pool/Client, or matches existing db helpers)
Migration is a SQL file (extension .sql) under db/migrations/
Migration includes both schema creation and at minimum an id + created_at column
Repository uses parameterized queries (e.g. $1 placeholders), not string interpolation
Does NOT introduce Mongo, Mongoose, MongoClient, or SQLite anywhere
Code is consistent with existing fixture style (CommonJS, async/await)
